title: Synchronize AgentBackgroundService shared mutable fields
description:
## Problem
`AgentBackgroundService.cs` has shared mutable fields accessed from multiple threads without synchronization:
- `_currentGoal` (line 171): Written in `SetGoal()`, `ProcessEventsAsync`, read via `CurrentGoal` property from REST API
- `_consecutiveFailures` (line 173): Written in `DispatchActionsAsync`, read via `ConsecutiveFailures` property
- `_connectionStatus` (line 182): Written in `ExecuteAsync`, read externally

Under concurrent access (event loop + REST API endpoint), readers may see stale or inconsistent state.

## Desired Outcome
All shared mutable fields are thread-safe.

## Scope
- Add `lock` or `Interlocked` to `_currentGoal`, `_consecutiveFailures`, `_connectionStatus`
- Convert `_connectionStatus` to an enum if still a string
- Add concurrent-access stress test

## Non-Goals
- Changing the overall architecture (single event loop is correct for event ordering)
- Synchronizing `_worldState` (covered by TSK-0187)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_currentGoal` updates are atomic (lock or `Interlocked.Exchange`)
- [ ] `_consecutiveFailures` uses `Volatile.Read/Write` or `Interlocked`
- [ ] `_connectionStatus` is thread-safe
- [ ] Concurrent-access stress test passes (read from REST endpoint while event loop writes)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
